subject: Ecology
title: Urban Flooding
description: Learn Urban Flooding in one minute.

hook.text: Cities flood more often and more violently than the landscapes they replaced.

buildup.text: Sealed surfaces accelerate runoff, overwhelming drains designed for lighter, older rainfall.

discovery.text: Climate change is intensifying downpours, delivering more water in shorter bursts.

twist.text: Flooding in cities disproportionately hits low-income neighborhoods built in low-lying areas.

climax.text: Sponge city designs—wetlands, parks, permeable ground—absorb surges before they cause damage.

punchline.text: A city that absorbs rain rarely drowns in it.

quiz.question: What is a sponge city?
options: A city designed to absorb rainwater naturally
